sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a sharp point (see שָׁמַר No. II .), hence thorn, collect. thorns, Isa 5:6 7:23 , 24 , 25 9:17 32:13 metaph. used of enemies, Isa 10:17 27:4 (Arab. سَمُرَ ةُ coll. سَمُرُ is the Egyptian thorn, a thorn-tree).

(2) diamond, so called from its cutting and perforating (as the point of a stylus was made of diamonds Jer 17:1), Eze 3:9 Zec 7:12. (Arab. سَامُورُ id. We may, perhaps, compare Gr. σμῖρις, σμῦρις, i.e. diamond dust, which was used in polishing. Bohlen considers the word to be of Indian origin, comparing asmîra, a stone which eats away, used of gems, iron.)

(3) [Shamir] pr.n.-(a) of a town in the tribe of Judah, Jos 15:48.-(b) of a town in mount Ephraim, Jud. 10:1 , Jdg 10:2.-(c) 1Ch 24:24 קרי where the כתיב has שָׁמוּר.
